user_request: I want to make these edits to the front end: Make sure all problem catagories are listed Top Down with their mastery percentage displayed. The file is math265-trainer.html Should be low number of phases.

qa_pairs.question: Should the mastery percentage be calculated from existing stats data (correct/incorrect attempts), or do you have a separate mastery tracking system already in place?
Do you want the problem categories listed as a vertical list (one per row) or can they remain as the current horizontal tab buttons, just with mastery percentages added to each?
Should the category list be sorted by mastery percentage (lowest to highest or vice versa), or should it maintain a fixed order (e.g., the current tab order)?

What defines 'difficulty order' for the categories? Should it be: (a) the current tab order in the HTML, (b) sorted by module name alphabetically, or (c) sorted by the user's current mastery percentage (lowest to highest or vice versa)?
Should the vertical category list replace the existing horizontal tab buttons entirely, or should it be a separate panel/sidebar while tabs remain for selection?
Do you want the mastery percentage to update in real-time as the user solves problems, or only when they return to the category selection screen?
Should the vertical category list show all modules (including locked ones), or only unlocked modules?
qa_pairs.answer: It should display locked and unlocked modules.

qa_pairs.question: What mastery percentage formula should be used: (correct / total_attempts * 100), or a different calculation like weighted recent performance?
Should clicking a category in the vertical list immediately start a problem from that module, or just select it for a 'Start' button?
qa_pairs.answer: Immediately start serving the user questions from that module.

phase_skeleton.project_title: Math265 Trainer - Vertical Category List with Mastery
phase_skeleton.project_goal: Frontend displays all problem categories as vertical list sorted alphabetically by module name, each showing real-time mastery percentage (correct/total*100), replacing horizontal tabs, clicking starts module immediately.

phases.title: Analyze Existing Structure
phases.goal: Understand current tab implementation and stats data flow. Identify all modules and how mastery data is stored.
phases.complexity_desc: Need to trace stats storage, tab rendering, and module selection logic in single large HTML file.
inputs: Interview requirements (vertical list, alphabetical order, real-time updates, locked+unlocked modules)
outputs: Documented location of module list, stats structure, and tab click handlers
List of all module identifiers and their display names
phases.validation_criteria: Can identify exact code sections for tab rendering, stats calculation, and module selection. Know where ALL_MODULES or equivalent is defined.
phases.key_risk: Single 2800-line file may have tightly coupled code making changes risky.

phases.title: Implement Vertical Category List
phases.goal: Replace horizontal tab buttons with vertical list component showing all modules alphabetically with mastery percentages.
phases.complexity_desc: Must refactor HTML structure, add mastery calculation logic, implement alphabetical sorting, and确保点击
inputs: Module list and stats structure from Phase 1
Requirements: vertical layout, alphabetical order, correct/total*100 formula, show locked+unlocked
outputs: New vertical category list UI component
Click handlers that immediately start module
phases.validation_criteria: Vertical list displays all modules in alphabetical order. Each shows current mastery percentage. Clicking starts serving questions from that module.
phases.key_risk: Breaking existing module selection flow or stats updates.

phases.title: Enable Real-Time Updates
phases.goal: Ensure mastery percentages update immediately when user solves problems without requiring page refresh.
phases.complexity_desc: Need to hook into existing stats update flow and trigger UI refresh after each problem submission.
inputs: Existing updateStats() function and call sites
outputs: Real-time mastery percentage updates on category list
Verified update flow from problem submission to UI refresh
phases.validation_criteria: Solving a problem immediately updates the mastery percentage shown in the category list without page reload.
phases.key_risk: Missing update call sites causing stale percentages.

qa_pairs.question: I found the module list at line 1711 and tab click handler at line 2605. Should the vertical list be implemented as a complete replacement of the existing tab buttons in the HTML (removing all tab-related markup), or should I keep the tab structure and just style it vertically?
qa_pairs.answer: Just Style it verically. Though I think some problem types need to be added to this list of modules.

qa_pairs.question: For the mastery percentage display, should it show as a text label (e.g., '45%') next to each module name, as a visual progress bar, or both?
qa_pairs.answer: Text label of a percentage only.

qa_pairs.question: The updateUnlockBar() function at line 1785 appears to handle stats updates. Should the real-time mastery percentage update be triggered by the same saveStore() call that happens after problem submission, or do you want a separate update mechanism?
You mentioned 'some problem types need to be added to this list of modules' - which specific modules/categories are currently missing from the tab list that should be included in the vertical list?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes you will need to review problem types produced by the backend.

qa_pairs.question: For locked modules, should they be visually distinguished (e.g., grayed out, different styling) in the vertical list, and can users click on locked modules or should they be non-interactive?

subphases.title: Map Tab HTML Structure
subphases.goal: Identify all tab button elements and their data-module attributes. Document exact line numbers and module identifiers. Goal achieved when all 10 current modules are listed with line references.
output_state: List of tab button line numbers (22-31)
List of module identifiers: factoring, exponents, fractions, trig, logs, composition, limits, derivatives, integration, adv_integration

subphases.title: Trace Stats Data Flow
subphases.goal: Locate MODULE_LABELS definition, updateUnlockBar() function, and saveStore() calls. Document how mastery percentages are calculated and when updates trigger. Goal achieved when stats update mechanism
input_state: List of module identifiers from subphase 101
output_state: Documented location of MODULE_LABELS (line 1905)
Documented updateUnlockBar() logic (line 1785)
Stats storage structure and update trigger points

subphases.title: Audit Backend Generators
subphases.goal: Review backend/generators/ directory to identify all problem types. Compare against current module list to find missing categories. Goal achieved when complete module list including new problem types
output_state: Complete list of all backend generator modules
List of missing modules to add to frontend
Final comprehensive module identifier list

subphases.title: Document Click Handlers
subphases.goal: Locate tab click event handlers and module selection logic. Document how clicking a tab loads problems. Goal achieved when click handler flow is fully mapped.
output_state: Documented click handler location and logic
Module selection and problem loading flow diagram

qa_pairs.question: Should locked modules show a lock icon or visual indicator in the vertical list, and should they be clickable or disabled?
qa_pairs.answer: Show a lock icon as a vidual indicator. Locked should not be clickable?

qa_pairs.question: What CSS styling approach should be used for the vertical list - should it match the existing tab button styling or use a new list-item style with hover states?
Should the mastery percentage display as a number (e.g., '75%'), a progress bar, or both - and where should it be positioned relative to the module name?

Should the vertical list be positioned in the same location as the current horizontal tabs (lines 22-31 in math265-trainer.html), replacing the button elements with a div container holding list items?
qa_pairs.answer: Position them closer to the left.

qa_pairs.question: For locked modules, should the lock icon appear before or after the module name text, and should the mastery percentage still be displayed for locked modules (even though they can't be clicked)?
qa_pairs.answer: Postion the percentage on the right, if the module is locked replace the % with the lock.

subphases.title: Mastery Calculation Function
subphases.goal: Create function that calculates mastery percentage from correct/total stats. Returns number for unlocked modules, lock indicator for locked modules.
input_state: Module list and stats structure from Phase 1
Requirements: correct/total*100 formula, locked module detection
output_state: Mastery percentage calculation function
Locked module detection logic
subphases.integration_risk: Calculation may use wrong stats fields or misidentify locked modules, causing incorrect percentage display.

subphases.title: Vertical List UI Component
subphases.goal: Replace horizontal tab buttons with vertical list container. Modules sorted alphabetically by name. Each item shows module name and mastery percentage/lock on right.
input_state: Requirements: vertical layout, alphabetical order, left positioning
output_state: New vertical category list UI component
Alphabetical sorting by module name
Lock icon for locked modules
subphases.integration_risk: HTML structure changes may break existing CSS or JavaScript that references tab elements.

subphases.title: Click Handlers and Module Start
subphases.goal: Implement click handlers for list items. Unlocked modules start immediately when clicked. Locked modules are not clickable.
input_state: Existing module start logic from Phase 1
Requirements: immediate module start, locked modules disabled
output_state: Click handlers that immediately start module
Locked module click prevention
subphases.integration_risk: Click handlers may not properly trigger module start or may allow clicking locked modules.

qa_pairs.question: Where in the codebase is problem submission handled, and does updateStats() get called immediately after each problem is answered, or is there a batch/delayed update mechanism?
qa_pairs.answer: I am unsure you will need to explore the code base. I didnt write the code.

qa_pairs.question: What mechanism should trigger the UI refresh after stats update: direct DOM manipulation of the category list elements, or is there a state management pattern (e.g., re-rendering a component) already in place?
qa_pairs.answer: Direct DOM manipulation (getElementById/querySelector and update textContent)

qa_pairs.question: Are there any other actions besides problem submission that should trigger mastery percentage updates (e.g., switching modules, restoring a session, undoing an answer)?
qa_pairs.answer: Only answer submission should trigger an update.

qa_pairs.question: What are the specific DOM selectors (IDs or classes) for the category list items and their mastery percentage elements that need to be updated?
Is updateStats() currently being called after problem submission, or do we need to add new call sites at the submission handler(s)?
qa_pairs.answer: You will need to review the code.

subphases.title: Explore Update Flow
subphases.goal: Identify all problem submission handlers and current updateStats() call sites. Document DOM selectors for category list mastery elements.
input_state: Existing updateStats() function and call sites
output_state: Documented submission handler locations
DOM selector map for mastery elements
Update flow analysis
subphases.integration_risk: Missing submission handlers or incorrect DOM selectors causes update failures

subphases.title: Hook Real-Time Updates
subphases.goal: Add updateStats() calls to submission handlers. Implement DOM update for mastery percentages. Verify end-to-end flow.
input_state: DOM selector map for mastery elements
output_state: Real-time mastery percentage updates on category list
Verified update flow from problem submission to UI refresh
subphases.integration_risk: Stale percentages if update call missed or DOM selector wrong

qa_pairs.question: The subphase output mentions tab buttons at lines 22-31, but the Phase 1 interview referenced line 1711 for the module list. Are the actual tab button HTML elements located around lines 22-31, or elsewhere in math265-trainer.html?
qa_pairs.answer: This requires you to review the code.

qa_pairs.question: Should this subphase document ONLY the existing 10 tab buttons currently in the HTML, or should I also identify which additional modules from the backend generators are missing from the current tab structure?
qa_pairs.answer: Document existing tabs AND note missing modules

qa_pairs.question: Do all tab buttons use the data-module attribute consistently, or are there any tabs with different attribute patterns (e.g., data-category, data-type) that I should account for?
qa_pairs.answer: I believe they are all the same type.

tasks.title: Locate Tab Buttons
tasks.description: Search math265-trainer.html for all button elements with data-module attributes. Document exact line numbers where each tab button appears. Extract the module identifier value from each data-module attribute. This creates the baseline inventory of current navigation structure.

output_files.description: Markdown document listing all tab buttons with line numbers and data-module values

constraints: Document exact line numbers as they appear in source
Include full data-module attribute value for each button
Note any variations in button structure or attributes

acceptance_criteria.Incomplete: No tab buttons documented or file not created
acceptance_criteria.In Progress: Some tab buttons documented but not all 10, or line numbers missing
acceptance_criteria.Complete: All 10 tab buttons documented with exact line numbers and data-module values

tests.behavior_description: Verify documented line numbers match actual button locations in source file
tests.expected_output: All 10 line numbers correctly reference button elements with data-module attributes

tasks.notes: Focus on buttons in navigation/tab area. May be around lines 22-31 or 1711 based on conflicting references.

tasks.title: Identify Missing Modules
tasks.description: Compare documented tab modules against backend/generators/ directory problem types. List which generator categories lack corresponding tab buttons. This identifies gaps between available problem types and user-accessible modules.

output_files.description: Append section listing missing modules that exist in generators but not in tab structure

constraints: Only list modules that have generator files but no tab button
Include generator filename for each missing module
Do not modify existing tab documentation section

acceptance_criteria.Incomplete: Missing modules section not added or comparison not performed
acceptance_criteria.In Progress: Some missing modules identified but generator directory not fully reviewed
acceptance_criteria.Complete: All missing modules documented with corresponding generator filenames

tests.behavior_description: Verify each listed missing module has a corresponding generator file and no tab button
tests.expected_output: All missing modules confirmed to have generator files without tab buttons

tasks.notes: Expected existing modules: factoring, exponents, fractions, trig, logs, composition, limits, derivatives, integration, adv_integration

qa_pairs.question: The scope context mentions MODULE_LABELS at line 1905 and updateUnlockBar() at line 1785 - should I document the exact variable names and data structures used for storing mastery stats (e.g., is it an object keyed by module ID, an array, or stored in localStorage)?

For the saveStore() calls that trigger updates, are there multiple call sites I should document (e.g., after each problem submission, on page load, on module switch), or just the primary one after problem completion?

Should the documented stats update mechanism include the exact code path from problem submission -> saveStore() -> updateUnlockBar() -> UI refresh, or just the key function locations and trigger points?
qa_pairs.answer: Full code path with intermediate function calls

tasks.title: Locate MODULE_LABELS Definition
tasks.description: Find MODULE_LABELS constant definition in math265-trainer.html. Document exact line number, variable structure, and how module IDs map to display labels. Identify data structure used for mastery stats storage (object keyed by module ID, array, or localStorage).

output_files.description: Document MODULE_LABELS location, structure, and mastery stats storage format

constraints: Document exact line numbers
Include full variable declaration
Note any related constants or config objects

acceptance_criteria.Incomplete: MODULE_LABELS location not identified
acceptance_criteria.In Progress: Location found but structure not fully documented

tests.behavior_description: Verify MODULE_LABELS line number matches actual code location
tests.expected_output: Documented line number matches actual MODULE_LABELS definition

tasks.notes: Cross-reference with module identifiers from subphase 101

tasks.title: Trace updateUnlockBar Logic
tasks.description: Analyze updateUnlockBar() function starting at line 1785. Document how mastery percentages are calculated, what inputs it receives, and how it updates the UI. Map all intermediate function calls within this flow.

output_files.description: Document updateUnlockBar() logic, calculation method, and UI update mechanism

constraints: Document all intermediate function calls
Include mastery percentage formula
Note any DOM elements modified

acceptance_criteria.Incomplete: updateUnlockBar() function not analyzed
acceptance_criteria.In Progress: Function logic partially documented
acceptance_criteria.Complete: Full calculation logic and UI update path documented

tests.behavior_description: Verify documented calculation matches actual code logic
tests.expected_output: Documented formula produces same results as code

tasks.notes: Check for any helper functions called within updateUnlockBar

tasks.title: Map saveStore Call Sites
tasks.description: Find all saveStore() call locations in the codebase. Document each trigger point (problem submission, page load, module switch). Map full code path from problem submission through saveStore to updateUnlockBar to UI refresh.

output_files.description: Document all saveStore() call sites and complete stats update code path

constraints: Document all call sites with line numbers
Include full call stack for each trigger
Note timing of updates (sync vs async)

acceptance_criteria.Incomplete: saveStore() call sites not identified
acceptance_criteria.In Progress: Some call sites documented but path incomplete
acceptance_criteria.Complete: All call sites and full update path documented

tests.behavior_description: Verify each documented call site exists at specified line
tests.expected_output: All documented saveStore() calls verified at correct locations

tasks.notes: Include any conditional logic that affects when saveStore is called

qa_pairs.question: What is the directory structure of backend/generators/? Is it flat (all generator files in one folder) or nested (subdirectories by category), and what file naming pattern identifies a problem type generator?
When comparing backend generators to frontend modules, should I match by filename, by an internal module ID within each generator file, or by some other identifier?

Beyond just listing the generator names, should I extract any metadata from each generator file (e.g., difficulty level, problem count, special flags) that would be useful for the vertical list implementation?

tasks.title: Inventory Generator Files
tasks.description: List all files in backend/generators/ directory. Record each filename, file path, and file size. This creates the baseline inventory of all available problem type generators. Match filenames to frontend modules by name.

output_files.description: Markdown table listing all generator files with paths and sizes

constraints: Record exact filenames including extensions
Include relative path from project root
Note any nested subdirectory structure

acceptance_criteria.Incomplete: Directory not fully scanned or inventory missing files
acceptance_criteria.In Progress: Inventory partially complete, some files documented
acceptance_criteria.Complete: All files in backend/generators/ listed with paths and sizes

tests.behavior_description: Verify inventory matches actual directory contents by cross-checking file count
tests.expected_output: File count in inventory matches actual directory file count

tasks.notes: Use ls -la or equivalent to capture all files including hidden ones

tasks.title: Extract Module Names
tasks.description: Read each generator file to extract the module identifier. Match by filename as confirmed in interview. Record the base filename (without extension) as the module identifier for comparison.

output_files.description: List of module identifiers extracted from generator filenames

constraints: Use base filename without extension as module ID
Preserve exact casing from filenames
Do not modify or normalize names

acceptance_criteria.Incomplete: Module names not extracted or extraction incomplete
acceptance_criteria.In Progress: Some module names extracted, process ongoing
acceptance_criteria.Complete: All generator files have corresponding module identifiers documented

tests.behavior_description: Verify each module name corresponds to an actual generator file
tests.expected_output: Every module name has a matching generator file in inventory

tasks.notes: Focus on filename only per interview guidance, no internal ID extraction needed

tasks.title: Compare Module Lists
tasks.description: Compare backend generator module names against frontend module list from Subphase 101. Identify modules present in backend but missing from frontend. Also note frontend modules without backend generators.

output_files.description: Comparison report showing missing modules in each direction

constraints: Use exact string matching for comparisons
Document both backend-missing and frontend-missing cases
Include count of gaps in each direction

acceptance_criteria.Incomplete: Comparison not performed or results incomplete
acceptance_criteria.In Progress: Comparison partially complete, some gaps identified
acceptance_criteria.Complete: All modules compared, gaps documented with counts

tests.behavior_description: Verify gap analysis by spot-checking random modules from each list
tests.input_data: Sample of 5 modules from each source list
tests.expected_output: Spot-check results match gap analysis documentation

tasks.notes: Reference task 10101 from Subphase 101 for frontend module list

tasks.title: Create Final Module List
tasks.description: Produce comprehensive module identifier list combining frontend and backend sources. Mark each module with source (frontend-only, backend-only, or both). This list drives vertical category implementation.

output_files.description: Final consolidated list of all modules with source indicators

constraints: Include source indicator for each module
Sort list alphabetically for readability
Include total count of modules

acceptance_criteria.Incomplete: Final list not created or missing source indicators
acceptance_criteria.In Progress: List partially compiled, consolidation ongoing
acceptance_criteria.Complete: Comprehensive list complete with all modules and source tags

tests.behavior_description: Verify final list contains all modules from gap analysis
tests.expected_output: Total module count matches sum from gap analysis

tasks.notes: This list becomes the authoritative source for vertical category implementation

qa_pairs.question: The Phase 1 interview mentioned a tab click handler at line 2605. Is this the only click handler location, or are there additional event listeners (e.g., event delegation on a parent container, or handlers registered elsewhere) that I should document?

When a tab is clicked, what is the exact function call sequence that loads problems? Does the click handler directly call a problem-loading function, or does it set state that triggers problem loading through a separate mechanism?

For locked modules, is there already logic in the click handler that prevents interaction, or is the locked state only visual (meaning I need to document where the click-blocking logic should be added)?

tasks.title: Locate Click Handlers
tasks.description: Search math265-trainer.html for all tab click event listeners. Document line numbers, handler function names, and event binding methods (onclick attributes, addEventListener, or jQuery). Identify if handlers use event delegation or direct binding.

output_files.description: Document all click handler locations with line numbers and binding methods

constraints: Document exact line numbers
Include all handler types (onclick, addEventListener, jQuery)
Note any conditional logic in handlers

acceptance_criteria.Incomplete: No handler locations documented
acceptance_criteria.In Progress: Some handlers found but incomplete line numbers or missing handler types
acceptance_criteria.Complete: All click handlers documented with exact line numbers, function names, and binding methods

tests.behavior_description: Verify documented line numbers match actual click handler code in source file
tests.expected_output: All documented line numbers correctly point to click handler code

tasks.notes: Check for both direct tab button handlers and any parent container delegation

tasks.title: Map Problem Loading Flow
tasks.description: Trace function call sequence from tab click to problem display. Document state changes, function calls, and any locked module checks. Create flow diagram showing complete click-to-problem-loading path.

output_files.description: Flow diagram and documentation of module selection and problem loading sequence

constraints: Include all function calls in sequence
Document any locked module prevention logic
Note async operations if present

acceptance_criteria.Incomplete: No flow documentation created
acceptance_criteria.In Progress: Partial flow documented but missing function calls or state changes
acceptance_criteria.Complete: Complete flow diagram with all function calls, state changes, and locked module checks documented

tests.behavior_description: Verify flow documentation matches actual code execution path when clicking tabs
tests.input_data: Browser console tracing of tab click events
tests.expected_output: Documented flow matches observed function call sequence

tasks.notes: Pay special attention to locked module handling - is it visual-only or does click handler block interaction

decisions.decision: Vertical module list will style existing tab buttons vertically rather than replacing HTML markup.
decisions.rationale: Preserves existing structure while achieving visual change with minimal refactoring.

decisions.decision: Mastery percentage will display as text label only, not as visual progress bar.
decisions.rationale: Simplifies UI implementation and reduces visual clutter in vertical list.

decisions.decision: Real-time mastery updates will use existing saveStore() trigger mechanism.
decisions.rationale: Leverages current architecture rather than adding separate update pathway.

decisions.decision: Locked modules will be visually distinguished and non-clickable.
decisions.rationale: Prevents user interaction with unavailable content while providing clear visual feedback.

decisions.decision: Backend generator matching will use filename as the module identifier.
decisions.rationale: Simplifies comparison logic by using consistent, predictable matching criteria.

decisions.decision: Generator file extraction will capture names only, not metadata like difficulty or flags.
decisions.rationale: Reduces scope to essential data needed for vertical list implementation.

decisions.decision: Stats documentation will trace full code path including intermediate function calls.
decisions.rationale: Provides complete understanding of data flow for accurate implementation.

decisions.decision: Subphase 101 will document existing tabs AND identify missing modules from backend.
decisions.rationale: Ensures comprehensive module inventory for complete vertical list.

qa_pairs.question: What is the exact function name and signature for the mastery calculation function, and where in math265-trainer.html should it be defined (e.g., in a script tag, as a method on an existing object)?
How should locked modules be detected - is there a specific flag/field in the stats object (e.g., stats.locked), a separate lockedModules array, or by checking if correct/total fields are missing?
What edge cases should the function handle: (a) total_attempts = 0 (division by zero), (b) missing stats object entirely, (c) negative values, or (d) all of the above?

tasks.title: Create Mastery Calculation Function
tasks.description: Build calculateMastery function that computes percentage from correct/total stats. Handle edge cases: total=0 returns 0, missing stats returns 0, negative values treated as 0. Formula: (correct/total)*100 rounded to integer. This function enables accurate mastery display for unlocked modules.

output_files.description: Add calculateMastery function in script section with edge case handling for division by zero, missing stats, and negative values

constraints: Function must be pure with no side effects
Return integer percentage (0-100)
Handle all edge cases without throwing errors
Maximum 50 lines of code

acceptance_criteria.Incomplete: Function skeleton exists but lacks edge case handling or calculation logic
acceptance_criteria.In Progress: Function calculates percentage correctly but missing some edge case handling
acceptance_criteria.Complete: Function handles all edge cases (total=0, missing stats, negative values) and returns correct integer percentage

tests.behavior_description: Function returns correct percentage when given valid correct/total values
tests.expected_output: Returns 80

tests.behavior_description: Function handles division by zero when total_attempts equals 0
tests.expected_output: Returns 0

tests.behavior_description: Function handles missing stats object gracefully
tests.expected_output: Returns 0

tasks.notes: Function signature: calculateMastery(stats) where stats has correct_attempts and total_attempts fields

tasks.title: Implement Locked Module Detection
tasks.description: Build isModuleLocked function that detects if module should be locked. Check for locked flag in stats or missing completion data. Return boolean for lock state. Integrate with calculateMastery to return lock indicator instead of percentage for locked modules. Enables proper UI state for locked vs unlocked modules.

output_files.description: Add isModuleLocked function and update calculateMastery to return lock indicator for locked modules

constraints: Function must be pure with no side effects
Locked modules return 'locked' string or null instead of percentage
Check for stats.locked flag or missing total_attempts
Maximum 50 lines of code

acceptance_criteria.Incomplete: isModuleLocked function exists but not integrated with calculateMastery
acceptance_criteria.In Progress: isModuleLocked works but calculateMastery still returns percentage for locked modules
acceptance_criteria.Complete: calculateMastery returns lock indicator for locked modules and percentage for unlocked modules

tests.behavior_description: Function detects locked module when stats.locked flag is true
tests.expected_output: Returns true for isModuleLocked, 'locked' for calculateMastery

tests.behavior_description: Function detects unlocked module when stats.locked flag is false or missing
tests.input_data: stats object with locked=false or no locked field, with valid attempts
tests.expected_output: Returns false for isModuleLocked, percentage for calculateMastery

tests.behavior_description: calculateMastery returns lock indicator for locked modules instead of percentage
tests.input_data: stats object with locked=true and correct=5, total=10
tests.expected_output: Returns 'locked' string instead of 50

tasks.notes: Lock indicator can be 'locked' string or null - choose based on how UI will consume the value

qa_pairs.question: The prior decisions state 'style existing tab buttons vertically rather than replacing HTML markup' but the scope says 'Replace horizontal tab buttons with vertical list container.' Should I modify the existing button elements to display vertically, or create a new div container with different markup (e.g., div/list-item structure)?
What is the exact function name for the mastery calculation from Subphase 201, and what parameter does it accept (module ID, module name, or stats object)?
For the lock icon on locked modules, should I use an existing icon library already in the project (e.g., Font Awesome), a Unicode character like 🔒, or an inline SVG?
qa_pairs.answer: I belive its a unicode character

tasks.title: Convert Tab Container to Vertical Layout
tasks.description: Modify existing tab button container CSS to display vertically instead of horizontally. Change flex-direction or display property to stack buttons. Maintain existing button styling while enabling vertical list appearance. This enables alphabetical module listing requirement.

Maintain backward compatibility with existing JavaScript selectors

Sort by module name string, case-insensitive

Display percentage as whole number with percent sign
Right-align mastery/lock indicator using flexbox

qa_pairs.answer: Use the exact same function/logic as existing tab handlers

decisions.decision: Locked modules display a Unicode lock icon (🔒) instead of mastery percentage.
decisions.rationale: Unicode character is already available in project, avoiding external icon library dependency.

decisions.decision: Locked modules are not clickable and show visual disabled state.
decisions.scope: Subphase 203: Click Handlers and Module Start
decisions.rationale: Prevents users from accessing content they haven't unlocked yet.

decisions.decision: Use existing tab button styling modified for vertical layout rather than new list-item markup.
decisions.rationale: Maintains visual consistency with existing UI and reduces CSS duplication.

decisions.decision: Mastery displays as number percentage only (e.g., '75%'), not progress bar.
decisions.rationale: Simpler implementation and cleaner visual presentation.

decisions.decision: Mastery percentage positioned on right side of module list item, lock icon replaces percentage for locked modules.
decisions.rationale: Consistent alignment and clear visual distinction between locked/unlocked state.

decisions.decision: Mastery calculation function handles all edge cases: division by zero, missing stats object, and negative values.
decisions.rationale: Ensures robust calculation regardless of data quality or missing information.

decisions.decision: Vertical list click handlers use exact same function/logic as existing tab handlers.
decisions.scope: Subphase 203: Click Handlers and Module Start
decisions.rationale: Maintains consistent module-start behavior and reduces code duplication.

decisions.decision: Vertical list positioned closer to the left rather than same location as horizontal tabs.
decisions.rationale: Better utilizes screen space for vertical list format.

decisions.decision: Modules sorted alphabetically by name before rendering in vertical list.
decisions.rationale: Provides predictable, findable ordering for users navigating modules.

qa_pairs.question: The existing updateStats() function currently updates the unlock bar. Should I modify updateStats() to also update the vertical category list mastery percentages, or create a separate updateCategoryList() function that gets called alongside updateStats() after problem submission?
qa_pairs.answer: Create separate updateCategoryList() function called after updateStats()

decisions.decision: Create separate updateCategoryList() function instead of modifying updateStats() to handle vertical category list mastery percentages.
decisions.rationale: Separation of concerns keeps updateStats focused on unlock bar while new function handles category list DOM updates.

decisions.decision: Only problem answer submission triggers mastery percentage updates, not module switching, session restore, or answer undo.
decisions.rationale: Limits update triggers to single action type, reducing complexity and potential update conflicts.

decisions.decision: Use direct DOM manipulation (getElementById/querySelector with textContent) for UI refresh instead of state management pattern.
decisions.rationale: Existing codebase uses direct DOM manipulation, maintaining consistency with current architecture.

decisions.decision: Mastery updates fire immediately after each problem submission, not via batch or delayed update mechanism.
decisions.rationale: Real-time feedback requirement demands immediate update rather than deferred processing.

decisions.decision: Vertical list module items use a DOM structure pattern other than button elements with data-module attributes.
decisions.rationale: Different selector pattern required for vertical category list versus original horizontal tabs.

decisions.decision: updateStats() will call updateCategoryList() after stats refresh to complete the real-time update flow.
decisions.rationale: Chaining the functions ensures category list updates happen after mastery data is refreshed.

project_plan.project_title: Math265 Trainer - Vertical Category List with Mastery
project_plan.project_goal: Frontend displays all problem categories as vertical list sorted alphabetically by module name, each showing real-time mastery percentage (correct/total*100), replacing horizontal tabs, clicking starts module immediately.

subsections.title: Mastery Calculation Function
subsections.goal: Create function that calculates mastery percentage from correct/total stats. Returns number for unlocked modules, lock indicator for locked modules.
input_state: Module list and stats structure from Phase 1
Requirements: correct/total*100 formula, locked module detection
output_state: Mastery percentage calculation function
Locked module detection logic
subsections.integration_risk: Calculation may use wrong stats fields or misidentify locked modules, causing incorrect percentage display.

tasks.title: Create Mastery Calculation Function
tasks.description: Build calculateMastery function that computes percentage from correct/total stats. Handle edge cases: total=0 returns 0, missing stats returns 0, negative values treated as 0. Formula: (correct/total)*100 rounded to integer. This function enables accurate mastery display for unlocked modules.

output_files.description: Add calculateMastery function in script section with edge case handling for division by zero, missing stats, and negative values

constraints: Function must be pure with no side effects
Return integer percentage (0-100)
Handle all edge cases without throwing errors
Maximum 50 lines of code

acceptance_criteria.Incomplete: Function skeleton exists but lacks edge case handling or calculation logic
acceptance_criteria.In Progress: Function calculates percentage correctly but missing some edge case handling
acceptance_criteria.Complete: Function handles all edge cases (total=0, missing stats, negative values) and returns correct integer percentage

tests.behavior_description: Function returns correct percentage when given valid correct/total values
tests.expected_output: Returns 80

tests.behavior_description: Function handles division by zero when total_attempts equals 0
tests.expected_output: Returns 0

tests.behavior_description: Function handles missing stats object gracefully
tests.expected_output: Returns 0

tasks.notes: Function signature: calculateMastery(stats) where stats has correct_attempts and total_attempts fields

output_files.description: Add isModuleLocked function and update calculateMastery to return lock indicator for locked modules

constraints: Function must be pure with no side effects
Locked modules return 'locked' string or null instead of percentage
Check for stats.locked flag or missing total_attempts
Maximum 50 lines of code

phases.title: Enable Real-Time Updates
phases.goal: Ensure mastery percentages update immediately when user solves problems without requiring page refresh.
phases.complexity_desc: Need to hook into existing stats update flow and trigger UI refresh after each problem submission.
inputs: Existing updateStats() function and call sites
outputs: Real-time mastery percentage updates on category list
Verified update flow from problem submission to UI refresh
phases.validation_criteria: Solving a problem immediately updates the mastery percentage shown in the category list without page reload.
phases.key_risk: Missing update call sites causing stale percentages.
